name: Tungiasis (Jigger Infestation)
description: An inflammatory skin ectoparasitosis caused by the burrowing of gravid female Tunga penetrans (sand fleas) into feet or hands. Causes severe pain, intense pruritus, secondary bacterial infections, and difficulty walking.

climateLinks.trigger: Dry Sandy Soils & Loose Dirt Floors
climateLinks.impact: Sand fleas multiply rapidly in dry, dusty soil during high-temperature seasons, especially in unpaved household dirt floors.

prevention: Paving loose dirt floors inside houses
Wearing closed shoes and washing feet regularly
Spraying homes with insecticide or applying neem seed oil barriers
triageAdvisory: Look for painful circular lesions with central black dots (flea respiratory stigmata) in periungual toe folds. Disinfect skin, carefully extract flea using sterile instruments, or smother locally using petroleum jelly.
